evals.prompt: I need a new generic Cortex-M firmware scaffold for a smart sensor node. I'm on macOS Apple Silicon. I do not know the final MCU yet, so create a build-smoke project in /tmp/embedded_eval_generic_sensor using the shared VS Code, clangd, clang-format, clang-tidy, CMakePresets.json, Ninja, arm-none-eabi-gcc, OpenOCD, Cortex-Debug, Docker, and GitHub Actions workflow. Do not generate STM32-specific GPIO register code.
evals.expected_output: A greenfield portable Cortex-M firmware project with generic firmware-debug/release/analyze presets, arm-none-eabi CMake toolchain, startup code, configurable linker script, portable BSP smoke code, VS Code Cortex-Debug/OpenOCD configs, Dockerfile, and GitHub Actions workflow.
expectations: CMakeLists.txt targets the arm-none-eabi-gcc toolchain and produces ELF, BIN, HEX, and MAP artifacts
cmake/arm-none-eabi-gcc.cmake sets CMAKE_SYSTEM_NAME Generic and uses arm-none-eabi-gcc
CMakePresets.json contains firmware-debug, firmware-release, and firmware-analyze presets
startup/startup.c provides a Cortex-M vector table and Reset_Handler
ldscripts/smart_sensor_node_flash.ld defines configurable FLASH and RAM origins and lengths
src/bsp/board.c is portable build-smoke code and does not contain STM32 RCC/GPIO register addresses
.vscode/launch.json has a Cortex-Debug configuration using OpenOCD
README.md mentions that OpenOCD target config must be set before flashing real hardware

evals.prompt: Create a Cortex-M firmware project in /tmp/embedded_eval_nrf52_logger for a custom nRF52840 data logger board. Target family nrf52, MCU nrf52840, define NRF52840_XXAA, Cortex-M4, 1024 KiB flash at 0x00000000, 256 KiB RAM at 0x20000000, FPU fpv4-sp-d16, hard float. Use J-Link with OpenOCD target nrf52 and name the presets with prefix nrf52. Include a layered application with app/, bsp/, and drivers/ directories, plus CMSIS/vendor SDK placeholders in the README.
evals.expected_output: A custom non-STM32 Cortex-M firmware scaffold with nrf52-debug/release/analyze presets, correct CPU/FPU/float-abi flags, linker memory map, OpenOCD J-Link/nrf52 config, portable BSP, and layered source tree.
expectations: CMakeLists.txt passes -mcpu=cortex-m4 -mthumb -mfpu=fpv4-sp-d16 -mfloat-abi=hard
CMakePresets.json has nrf52-debug, nrf52-release, and nrf52-analyze presets
ldscripts/nrf52_logger_flash.ld has flash origin 0x00000000, 1024K flash, RAM origin 0x20000000, and 256K RAM
.vscode/launch.json references interface/jlink.cfg and target/nrf52.cfg
src/ contains app/, bsp/, and drivers/ subdirectories
README.md notes the nRF52840 target, custom board, and CMSIS/vendor SDK integration path

evals.prompt: Modernize an existing Cortex-M firmware project in /tmp/embedded_eval_lpc_legacy. It currently has a vendor IDE project, LPC1768 startup/linker files, a small drivers folder, and a J-Link debug setup. Convert it to CMake + Ninja + arm-none-eabi-gcc, add clang-format/clang-tidy, OpenOCD flash/debug scripts, VS Code Cortex-Debug, Docker, and GitHub Actions. Preserve the existing board bring-up and vendor driver code.
evals.expected_output: The existing LPC1768 firmware is modernized with CMake, presets, toolchain file, clang-format/tidy, OpenOCD scripts, VS Code configs, Docker, and CI while keeping chip-specific startup/linker and board driver code intact.
expectations: Existing startup, linker, board, and driver files are preserved, not deleted
CMakeLists.txt is added or updated for arm-none-eabi-gcc and Ninja
cmake/arm-none-eabi-gcc.cmake is created or reused
CMakePresets.json uses target-neutral or lpc-specific debug, release, and analyze presets instead of STM32-only names
.clang-format, .clang-tidy, and .clangd are added
.vscode/launch.json uses Cortex-Debug with OpenOCD and J-Link
scripts/flash.sh and scripts/openocd_server.sh are added
README.md documents the new build/flash workflow and target-specific memory/debug assumptions
